question.text: If Rani postpones her retirement from age 60 to age 65 (30 years to retirement instead of 25), her required monthly savings at 12% return drops to approximately:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** With 30 years to retirement (360 months) instead of 25 years (300 months), the required monthly savings drops to approximately Rs.8,512. The longer accumulation period and compounding effect significantly reduce the monthly burden. **The Trap:** One might think postponing by 5 years would only slightly reduce the savings. In reality, the extra 5 years of compounding at 12% cuts the required savings nearly in half. **Cross-Exam Context:** This illustrates the power of compounding and time value of money — a concept tested repeatedly across NISM modules. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Use this comparison to motivate young clients to start early.
